purpose: *** Complete traceability: Root Cause -> Slice -> Permitted Mutation Surface -> Verification -> Rollback -> Approval -> Completion -> Architectural Regeneration -> CI Enforcement (Cycle 7). NO ORPHANED ROOT CAUSES. NO SLICE WITHOUT ARCHITECTURAL JUSTIFICATION. NO VERIFICATION THAT DOES NOT TRACE TO AN INVARIANT. ***

THE_ORPHAN_CYCLE_4_LEFT.finding: *** Cycle 4 has TEN root causes (RC-1..RC-10) and TEN slices (S01..S10). It LOOKS like a bijection. IT IS NOT. ***

the_arithmetic.RC-4 + RC-5: -> S01 (TWO root causes COLLAPSE into one slice)

THE_ORPHAN_CYCLE_4_LEFT.cycle_4s_own_words: 'It does not fix RC-9 (the mutation/validator boundary). Zero reachability. Filed, sequenced last.'
THE_ORPHAN_CYCLE_4_LEFT.was_cycle_4_wrong_to_defer_it: *** NO. Deferring it was CORRECT. Fixing RC-9 means touching 8 models and ~57 model_copy sites to close a bug with ZERO CURRENT REACHABILITY, while RC-4 silently deletes media. Cycle 4's own words: 'fixing it first would spend the audit's credibility on a bug nobody can hit.' THAT JUDGEMENT STANDS. ***
THE_ORPHAN_CYCLE_4_LEFT.but: *** The Cycle-6 completion standard states: 'There must be no orphaned root causes.' A deferral is not a discharge. And 'we checked, it's unreachable' is a claim with a SHELF LIFE — it was true in Cycle 2, Cycle 3, Cycle 4 and Cycle 5, and NOTHING IN THE REPOSITORY PREVENTS IT FROM BECOMING FALSE IN THE NEXT PR. ***
THE_ORPHAN_CYCLE_4_LEFT.the_resolution: *** S11 — a GUARD slice. It does NOT fix RC-9. It PINS RC-9's unreachability in CI, so a fifth door cannot be added silently. Test-only. Zero blast radius. Self-merges on green. IT IS THE CHEAPEST DISCHARGE OF AN ORPHANED ROOT CAUSE AVAILABLE, and it is the reason the recommended landing order puts it SECOND — before S03/S04/S06, all three of which mutate `Post`. ***

root_cause_to_completion.why_this_discharges_the_orphan: *** The root cause is not FIXED — and the contract does not pretend it is. It is CONTAINED: it can no longer become reachable without CI going red. That converts an inspection result with a shelf life into a mechanical invariant. It is the right-sized response to a CRITICAL-if-reached defect with ZERO reachability. ***

risks_that_are_NOT_root_causes_and_there.statement: *** Import-time acyclicity is PURCHASED, not designed. 107 of 323 lazy edges point to an equal-or-higher level; 56 are STRICTLY UPWARD. Module init order is LOAD-BEARING and NOTHING ENFORCES IT. ***
risks_that_are_NOT_root_causes_and_there.discovered_by: Cycle 5 — *** AFTER Cycle 4 designed the slices. NOT ONE OF THE TEN C4 PROMPTS FORBIDS HOISTING A LAZY IMPORT. ***
handled_as: *** GB-1 — a MANDATORY GLOBAL PRESERVATION BOUNDARY on all 11 slices. This requires NO approval: it FORBIDS a change, it does not make one. ***

*** S12 — a PROPOSED GUARD slice that makes GB-1 mechanical. THIS REQUIRES APPROVAL (PD-5), because AR-04 is a RISK, not an approved ROOT CAUSE, and building it unbidden would be the exact hidden scope expansion this contract exists to prevent. ***
risks_that_are_NOT_root_causes_and_there.the_distinction_that_matters: *** A boundary that FORBIDS is free. A guard that ENFORCES is code. The first is inherited from the architecture; the second is a decision. Cycle 6 takes the first and SURFACES the second. ***

risks_that_are_NOT_root_causes_and_there.handled_as: *** OUT OF SCOPE. A RECORDED, ACCEPTED decision (studio/CLAUDE.md: 'no auth by design ... declined as out-of-scope for localhost'). Re-raise as a PRODUCT question, not a bug. NO SLICE. ***

*** OUT OF SCOPE. Single-process by design today. Self-declared in post/CLAUDE.md. ***

completeness_assertions.every_root_cause_maps_to_at_least_one_sl: *** TRUE — RC-1:S03 · RC-2:S04+S05 · RC-3:S02 · RC-3b:S07 · RC-4:S01 · RC-5:S01 · RC-6:S08 · RC-7:S10(BLOCKED) · RC-8:S06+S04 · RC-9:S11(GUARD) · RC-10:S09. ZERO ORPHANS. ***
completeness_assertions.every_slice_traces_to_an_approved_root_c: *** TRUE for S01-S11. S12 does NOT — and it is therefore marked PROPOSED and gated on PD-5 rather than smuggled into the program. ***
completeness_assertions.every_slice_has_a_rollback_class: *** TRUE. And TWO of them are NOT simply 'revert' — S02 (WORLD_IRREVERSIBLE, conditional, condition does not fire today) and S04 (DATA_IRREVERSIBLE, residue empty today). Cycle 4 wrote 'revert' for both. ***
completeness_assertions.implementation_ordering_is_acyclic: *** TRUE. Proven by construction: 4 ordering edges, 0 back edges. The two co-requirements (S04<->S07, S04<->S06) are modelled as CO-REQUIREMENTS, not ordering edges — which is what keeps the graph honest rather than hand-waving a cycle away. ***
